name: Eddie Henderson
abstract: Eddie Henderson (born October 26, 1940) is an American jazz trumpet and flugelhorn player. He came to prominence in the early 1970s as a member of pianist Herbie Hancock's Mwandishi band, going on to lead his own electric/fusion groups through the decade.

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: I told Eddie Henderson – I said, “Eddie, if we ever get the Jazztet together again, you’re going to be my trumpet player.” ... But I didn’t foresee it. But it happened, and he’s my trumpet player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: I get with Eddie Henderson, start working with him and Julian Priester. We worked six months in a club.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: So I changed trumpet players after that. It was Eddie, Eddie Henderson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: Norman Connors... had all these all-star bands, Ernie, yourself, everybody on them... Eddie Henderson, Gary Bartz... everybody. Then he kind of went left... Here’s the second one – another – probably the third one. Some of the same folks... Eddie Henderson, yourself, Gary Bartz...
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David Friesen
about_quotes.evidence: I remember Eddie Henderson called me when Head Hunters were getting ready to play, and he wanted me to play the bass.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Friesen interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 24, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Davis
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Henderson, the great trumpeter, he said to me once ... he leaned over to me and said
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Davis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, December 15, 2017
